title: Basic use of command lines
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8l330","text":"Learn the basic commands to navigate in a command-line terminal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Note : This protocol requires you to have a command-line interface installed on your laptop. Please read the 'Before starting' section in 'Guidelines and Warnings' of this protocol to learn more about the installation. For Windows users : Launch Cygwin terminal from the application menu For MacOS users : Launch iTerm2, and open a new window. For linux (Ubuntu users) : Open the Dash by clicking the Ubuntu icon in the upper-left, type 'terminal', and select the Terminal application from the results that appear. You can also use the shortcut Alt+tab+T.

Here are the most common command lines that you will need to use. Those commands work on Cygwin, iTerm and Linux shell. %s %s Other important shortcuts : %s %s shortcut example effect .. cd .. Directory parent to the current one. The command 'cd ..' will change the user's current working directory one one level up. . ./programA Current directory. The command './programA' will execute the file 'programA' located in the user's current working directory.
creator: Alise J Ponsero
